rationale: DIM1=1: Treatment case; output p=55, which falls in 50<=p<70 band. DIM2=2: Top-ranked hypothesis names revenue (SalesRevenueServicesNet) and AR accounts, direction overstated, treatment 'premature or aggressive revenue recognition (billing ahead of service delivery)' plus under-provisioned bad-debt allowance — this substantially matches the enforcement description (premature recognition of revenue for unperformed work; bad-debt reserve violations). Not a 3: no case-specific pinpointed fact from the key is named (no ~$40M/$566M magnitude, customer prepayments booked as revenue, contingent-fee revenue, business partner's loan recorded as revenue, or falsified records to auditors). Coverage: hypothesis 2 also hits the bad-debt reserve element but places it in 2010-2012, before the 2013-01 manipulation start; loan-as-revenue and document forgery are uncovered. DIM3: Top hypothesis maps to 'mixed' (active premature recognition + omission-estimate under-reserving), but the answer key's genre_tag_row is null, so there is no key genre to compare against; score set to null for the human auditor to finalize. DIM4=3: No cap — p=55 with risk_tier 'watch' satisfies the consistency rule. Evidence cites specific provided data points that genuinely support the mechanism claims: AR 43,273k->56,948k (+31.6%) vs revenue 188,914k->212,476k (+12.5%) FY2013->FY2014 (accession 0001047469-15-002289); AR +16.3% vs revenue +3.8% through Q3 2015 (0001104659-15-077269); allowance 245k->102k while AR grew 14,295k->33,223k (2010-2012). These are combined coherently across multiple years and quarterly points (annual AR/revenue divergence, allowance-ratio decline, Q2-Q3 2015 loss swing) qualifying for 3. Noted weakness (does not defeat the score): CL3 flags NI-vs-OCF 'divergence' where OCF exceeds NI (e.g., FY2013 NI 4,962k vs OCF 21,413k), which is directionally the benign pattern. MEMORIZATION_COND2=false: reasoning anchors on concrete provided data values throughout (not mere document listing) and mentions no post-cutoff facts — no reference to the SEC complaint, LR-24255, restatement, or enforcement outcome; hypothesis 3 explicitly reasons only from pre-cutoff data.
